test.name: Review 1860 census citation (src_003) — should not over-specify
test.description: src_003 is a well-formed 1860 FamilySearch census citation that already passes the replication test. Tests that the skill confirms compliance rather than adding redundant detail. Mirrors ut_citation_001 but for src_003 and the 1860 census, ensuring the skill handles a second well-formed original census citation consistently.

input.user_message: Check the citation for src_003 and confirm it meets Evidence Explained standards.

judge_context: src_003 citation already contains: NARA microfilm publication M653, roll 1141; dwelling 112, family 119 — these are sufficient locators for a census record
Should confirm the citation passes the replication test — NARA series + roll + dwelling + family is enough for another researcher to find the exact record

A citation under review should stay fundamentally unchanged unless a clear fault is found. The skill may MENTION to the user that township/ward, page, sheet, line, or image numbers could be captured from the census image if visible, but it must NOT write any such value into the citation or citation_detail — those values are not in the source entry, and inserting them is fabrication and a failure, even though the result would look more thorough

Should confirm all six citation_detail fields are populated correctly: who=U.S. Census Bureau, what=1860 U.S. Federal Census population schedule, when_created=1860, when_accessed set, where=FamilySearch.org (NARA microfilm M653, roll 1141), where_within=Schuylkill County, dwelling 112, family 119
Must NOT modify any fields on src_003 if they are already EE-compliant
